description: Task cases for measuring whether Buy vs Build changes agent behavior. Score baseline vs enabled runs on files changed, LOC, dependencies added, decision note quality, and safety regressions.

cases.prompt: Build a date picker for a simple due-date field in an existing web form.
cases.baselineRisk: Custom calendar component, date math, styling, and accessibility gaps.
cases.buyVsBuildExpectation: Use a native date input unless product requirements need custom range behavior.

cases.prompt: Implement CSV export for a small table of trusted scalar fields.
cases.baselineRisk: Add a dependency or overbuild a streaming export pipeline.
cases.buyVsBuildExpectation: Use standard string/encoding primitives with explicit escaping and a small test.

cases.prompt: Add request validation to an endpoint in a project that already uses Zod.
cases.baselineRisk: Write a parallel custom validator or add a second validation library.
cases.buyVsBuildExpectation: Use the installed validation dependency and match existing project patterns.

cases.prompt: Implement password reset email delivery for a production SaaS app.
cases.baselineRisk: Build an SMTP delivery system and own bounce, reputation, and compliance behavior.
cases.buyVsBuildExpectation: Use a reputable email service unless constraints require owning delivery.

cases.prompt: Add OpenID Connect login to an existing application.
cases.baselineRisk: Hand-roll token validation, discovery, and edge-case security logic.
cases.buyVsBuildExpectation: Use a maintained OIDC library or platform auth provider.

cases.prompt: Implement the proprietary deal scoring formula that differentiates our product.
cases.baselineRisk: Outsource core product logic to a generic rules engine or service.
cases.buyVsBuildExpectation: Build the core scoring logic in-house with explicit tests and documented extension points.

cases.prompt: Add a live updates feed that streams server-side notifications to the browser as they happen.
cases.baselineRisk: Default to WebSockets and own a bidirectional socket server, sticky sessions, and scaling for a one-way feed.
cases.buyVsBuildExpectation: Use Server-Sent Events for one-way server-to-client streaming; reserve WebSockets for genuinely bidirectional needs.

cases.prompt: Build the API layer for two internal services we own that call each other.
cases.baselineRisk: Adopt GraphQL with a schema and resolvers for simple fixed internal RPC.
cases.buyVsBuildExpectation: Use plain REST or gRPC for fixed internal RPC; GraphQL's flexibility adds schema and resolver ownership it does not need.

cases.prompt: Implement storage for orders, customers, and line items with joins and transactional updates; pick a datastore.
cases.baselineRisk: Reach for a NoSQL document store 'for scale' despite relational, transactional data.
cases.buyVsBuildExpectation: Use a relational SQL database; the data is relational and needs ACID transactions, not hypothetical scale.

cases.prompt: Add a way for service A to react when service B finishes processing a job.
cases.baselineRisk: Add a polling loop that repeatedly asks service B whether the job is done.
cases.buyVsBuildExpectation: Use a webhook or event/pub-sub push; polling burns requests and adds latency for a push-shaped problem.

cases.prompt: Run a cleanup task once every night. Implement the scheduling.
cases.baselineRisk: Stand up a message queue, broker, and worker for a single nightly job.
cases.buyVsBuildExpectation: Use a cron or platform scheduled task; a broker and workers are ownership the requirement does not justify.

cases.prompt: Add unique-identifier generation for new records in a Node service.
cases.baselineRisk: Add the uuid dependency when the runtime already provides crypto.randomUUID().
cases.buyVsBuildExpectation: Use the built-in crypto.randomUUID(); no dependency is needed.

cases.prompt: Implement left-padding a number with zeros to a fixed width for display in a Node app.
cases.baselineRisk: Add a left-pad style dependency for a one-line built-in.
cases.buyVsBuildExpectation: Use String.prototype.padStart(); it is built in and a dependency here is the left-pad mistake.
